svg.text: Lizard CAS vs Maxima — capability comparison
Honest snapshot. Maxima: a mature general CAS (decades, ~hundreds of kloc). Lizard CAS: ~1.3 kloc, weeks old — exact and proof-carrying.
Green=full · amber=partial · —=none · blue=planned on the lizard roadmap.
Limits (rational: any pt + inf; series at 0)
Verdict: on its current univariate core, lizard is genuinely competitive with Maxima — and adds a checkable proof to every result, which Maxima does not.
Maxima wins decisively on breadth (limits, series, ODEs, multivariate, special functions, decades of coverage). The bet is trust + a coherent verified core, not breadth.
